core_mechanism: two-path coherent interferometry: a single coherent wave amplitude is split into two spatially separated branches, a controllable relative phase is accumulated between them, and that phase is read out from the fringe pattern produced when the two amplitudes are recombined and interfere
brief_reason: The abstract coherently splits one wave into a double-well (two branches), lets a controllable relative phase evolve (tuned by ac Stark shifts), and recovers that phase from the spatial interference pattern of the overlapping matter waves — the defining structure of a two-path interferometer.
